verses.sanskrit: karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣhu kadācana | mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stvakarmaṇi
verses.english: You have a right to perform your prescribed duties, but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions.

verses.sanskrit: dhyāyato viṣhayān puṅsaḥ saṅgas teṣhūpajāyate | saṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate
verses.english: While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them, and from such attachment lust develops, and from lust anger arises.

verses.sanskrit: na jāyate mriyate vā kadāchin nāyaṃ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ | ajo nityaḥ śāśvato ’yaṃ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre
verses.english: The soul is neither born, nor does it ever die; nor having once existed, does it ever cease to be. The soul is without birth, eternal, immortal, and ageless.

verses.sanskrit: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṃ kāryaṃ karma samāchara | asakto hyācharan karma param āpnoti pūruṣhaḥ
verses.english: Therefore, giving up attachment, perform actions as a matter of duty because by working without being attached to fruits, one attains the Supreme.

verses.sanskrit: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata | abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṃ sṛijāmyaham
verses.english: Whenever there is a decline in righteousness and an increase in unrighteousness, O Bharat, at that time I manifest myself on earth.

verses.sanskrit: paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṃ vināśhāya cha duṣhkṛitām | dharma-saṃsthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge
verses.english: To protect the righteous, to annihilate the wicked, and to reestablish the principles of dharma, I appear on this earth, age after age.

verses.sanskrit: chātur-varṇyaṃ mayā sṛiṣhṭaṃ guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśhaḥ | tasya kartāram api māṃ viddhyakartāram avyayam
verses.english: The four categories of occupations were created by me according to people's qualities and activities.

verses.sanskrit: vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini | śhuni chaiva śhva-pāke cha paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśhinaḥ
verses.english: The truly learned, with the eyes of divine knowledge, see with equal vision a Brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and a dog-eater.

verses.sanskrit: uddhared ātmanātmānaṃ nātmānam avasādayet | ātmaiva hyātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ
verses.english: Elevate yourself through your own mind, and not degrade yourself. For the mind can be the friend and also the enemy of the self.

verses.sanskrit: bandhur ātmātmanas tasya yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥ | anātmanas tu śhatrutve vartetātmaiva śhatru-vat
verses.english: For those who have conquered the mind, it is their best friend. For those who have failed to do so, the mind remains their greatest enemy.

verses.sanskrit: manuṣhyāṇāṃ sahasreṣhu kaśhchid yatati siddhaye | yatatām api siddhānāṃ kaśhchin māṃ vetti tattvataḥ
verses.english: Amongst thousands of persons, hardly one strives for perfection; and amongst those who have achieved perfection, hardly one knows me in truth.

verses.sanskrit: anta-kāle cha māmeva smaran muktvā kalevaram | yaḥ prayāti sa mad-bhāvaṃ yāti nāstyatra sanśhayaḥ
verses.english: Those who relinquish the body while remembering me alone even at the moment of death, attain my nature. Of this, there is no doubt.

verses.sanskrit: ananyāśh chintayanto māṃ ye janāḥ paryupāsate | teṣhāṃ nityābhiyuktānāṃ yoga-kṣhemaṃ vahāmyaham
verses.english: There are those who always worship me with fixed attention. For those who are always absorbed in me, I carry what they lack and preserve what they have.

verses.sanskrit: patraṃ puṣhpaṃ phalaṃ toyaṃ yo me bhaktyā prayachchhati | tadahaṃ bhaktyupahṛitam aśhnāmi prayatātmanaḥ
verses.english: If one offers me with love and devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or water, I will accept it.

verses.sanskrit: yat karoṣhi yad aśhnāsi yaj juhoṣhi dadāsi yat | yat tapasyasi kaunteya tat kuruṣhva mad-arpaṇam
verses.english: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer as sacrifice, whatever you give in charity, and whatever penance you perform—do that as an offering to me.

verses.sanskrit: ahaṃ sarvasya prabhavo mattaḥ sarvaṃ pravartate | iti matvā bhajante māṃ budhā bhāva-samanvitāḥ
verses.english: I am the source of all spiritual and material worlds. Everything emanates from me. The wise who perfectly know this engage in my devotional service.

verses.sanskrit: teṣhāṃ satata-yuktānāṃ bhajatāṃ prīti-pūrvakam | dadāmi buddhi-yogaṃ taṃ yena mām upayānti te
verses.english: To those who are constantly devoted and worship me with love, I give the understanding by which they can come to me.

verses.sanskrit: tasmāt tvam uttiṣhṭha yaśho labhasva jitvā śhatrūn bhuṅkṣhva rājyaṃ samṛiddham | mayaivete nihatāḥ pūrvam eva nimitta-mātraṃ bhava savya-sāchin
verses.english: Therefore, arise! Win glory, conquer your enemies, and enjoy a prosperous kingdom. These warriors have already been slain by me; be you only an instrument, O Savyasachin.

verses.sanskrit: adveṣhṭā sarva-bhūtānāṃ maitraḥ karuṇa eva cha | nirmamo nirahankāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣhamī
verses.english: Those who are free from malice toward all living beings, who are friendly and compassionate, free from attachment and egoism, balanced in pleasure and pain, and ever-forgiving.

verses.sanskrit: samaṃ paśhyan hi sarvatra samavasthitam īśhvaram | na hinastyātmanātmānaṃ tato yāti parāṃ gatim
verses.english: Seeing the same Supreme Lord residing in all beings, one does not degrade the self by the self, and thus attains the supreme destination.

verses.sanskrit: māṃ cha yo ’vyabhichāreṇa bhakti-yogena sevate | sa guṇān samatītyaitān brahma-bhūyāya kalpate
verses.english: Those who serve me with unswerving devotion transcend the three modes of material nature and rise to the level of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: mamaivāṃśho jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ | manaḥ-ṣhaṣhṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛiti-sthāni karṣhati
verses.english: The embodied souls in this material world are my eternal fragmental parts. Having come into the material nature, they are struggling with the six senses, including the mind.

verses.sanskrit: sarvasya chāhaṃ hṛidi sanniviṣhṭo mattaḥ smṛitir jñānam apohanaṃ cha | vedaiśh cha sarvair aham eva vedyo vedānta-kṛid veda-vid eva chāham
verses.english: I am seated in the hearts of all living beings. From me come memory, knowledge, as well as forgetfulness. I am alone to be known by all the Vedas, and I am the author of Vedant, and the knower of the meaning of the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: tri-vidhaṃ narakasyedaṃ dvāraṃ nāśhanam ātmanaḥ | kāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayaṃ tyajet
verses.english: There are three gates leading to the hell of self-destruction for the soul—lust, anger, and greed. Therefore, one should abandon these three.

verses.sanskrit: sattvānurūpā sarvasya śhraddhā bhavati bhārata | śhraddhā-mayo ’yaṃ puruṣho yo yach-chhraddhaḥ sa eva saḥ
verses.english: The faith of all humans conforms to the nature of their minds. All people possess faith, and whatever the nature of their faith, that is what they are.

verses.sanskrit: yataḥ pravṛittir bhūtānāṃ yena sarvam idaṃ tatam | sva-karmaṇā tam abhyarchya siddhiṃ vindati mānavaḥ
verses.english: By performing one's natural occupation, one worships the Creator from whom all living entities have come into being, and by whom the whole universe is pervaded.

verses.sanskrit: īśhvaraḥ sarva-bhūtānāṃ hṛid-deśhe ’rjuna tiṣhṭhati | bhrāmayan sarva-bhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā
verses.english: The Supreme Lord dwells in the hearts of all living beings, O Arjuna. According to their karmas, he directs the wanderings of the souls, who are seated as on a machine made of material energy.

verses.sanskrit: iti te jñānam ākhyātaṃ guhyād guhyataraṃ mayā | vimarśhyaitad aśheṣheṇa yathechchhasu tathā kuru
verses.english: Thus, I have revealed to you this knowledge that is more secret than all secrets. Ponder over it deeply, and then do as you wish.

verses.sanskrit: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṃ śharaṇaṃ vraja | ahaṃ tvā sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣhayiṣhyāmi mā śhuchaḥ
verses.english: Abandon all varieties of dharmas and simply surrender unto me alone. I shall liberate you from all sinful reactions; do not fear.

verses.sanskrit: naṣhṭo mohaḥ smṛitir labdhā tvat-prasādān mayāchyuta | sthito ’smi gata-sandehaḥ kariṣhye vachanaṃ tava
verses.english: My delusion is gone and my memory has been restored by your grace. I am now firm and free from doubt, and I am prepared to act according to your instructions.
